mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized demand recovery across services and durable assets, generating positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising industrial production signals resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative 5d returns in > 50% of tightening regime observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns exceed 55% of instances when inflation accelerates past the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based demand strength, reducing recession probabilities and generating positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20d returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances following a production contraction exceeding the threshold.
